settings.archivedConversations.title: Archived Conversations
settings.archivedConversations.descripti: Archived conversations no longer appear in the left navigation. Review and restore them here.
settings.archivedConversations.empty.des: After you archive a conversation from the left conversation menu, it leaves navigation and appears here.
settings.appearance.language.title: Interface Language
settings.appearance.language.tip: Only changes the interface display language. It does not affect reply language, browser site language, or content target language.
settings.language.auto.hint: Follow the system interface language, falling back to Simplified Chinese when unsupported.
settings.language.en-US.hint: Best for an English UI and terminology environment.
settings.language.zh-TW.hint: Best for Traditional Chinese interface reading environments.
settings.language.ja-JP.hint: Best for a Japanese UI and Japan market tasks.
settings.language.ko-KR.hint: Best for a Korean UI and Korea market tasks.
settings.about.update.errorCheck: Unable to check for updates right now. Please try again later.
settings.about.update.errorDownload: Unable to install the update automatically. Please download the latest version from the web page.
settings.about.update.progress.upToDate: You are on the latest version
settings.about.version.label: Version {{version}} ({{build}})
settings.about.status.latest: You are on the latest version
settings.about.windowsSetupNotice: Windows provides a single setup installer. To upgrade manually or reinstall, use the latest version from the web download page.
settings.profile.field.nickname.label: Nickname
settings.profile.field.nickname.descript: The name shown in the workspace and AI context.
settings.profile.field.nickname.hint: Use the name you prefer so Lime can show it consistently.
settings.profile.field.bio.label: Bio
settings.profile.field.bio.description: Summarize your work background, interests, and current goals.
settings.profile.field.bio.placeholder: Add your role, focus, or current project so AI can understand you faster.
settings.profile.field.bio.hint: Press Enter to save, or Shift + Enter for a new line.
settings.profile.field.email.label: Email
settings.profile.field.email.description: Account email. This page only displays it and cannot edit it directly.
settings.profile.field.email.hint: Use the account entry or a dedicated flow to change your email later.
settings.profile.message.configPending: Profile settings are still loading and cannot be saved yet.
settings.profile.message.fileTooLarge: File is too large ({{size}}MB). Maximum size is {{max}}MB.
settings.profile.message.avatarWip: Avatar upload is still being refined.
settings.profile.hero.title: Profile
settings.profile.hero.tip: Manage your nickname, bio, email, and preference tags so Lime can understand your workspace context.
settings.profile.hero.subtitle: Manage your nickname, bio, email, and preference tags.
settings.profile.avatar.bioEmpty: Add a few lines about your work background, focus, or goals so AI enters the right context faster.
settings.profile.avatar.tagsEmpty: Add tags to help AI understand your domain preferences
settings.profile.avatar.limitInline: Avatar format limits are in Tips
settings.profile.avatar.limitTip: Avatar supports PNG / JPG / GIF / WEBP, up to 5MB per file.
settings.profile.basic.title: Basic Profile
settings.profile.basic.tip: These details can inform greetings, workspace naming, and personalized prompts.
settings.profile.tags.title: Preference Tags
settings.profile.tags.tip: Tags help AI infer topics and expression styles you care about.
settings.profile.tags.empty: No tags yet. Start from suggested tags or add a custom domain.
settings.profile.tags.customPlaceholder: For example: full-stack engineering, content strategy, productivity tools
settings.profile.usage.title: How Profile Is Used
settings.profile.usage.tipNickname: Nickname and bio help AI naturally reference your background in openings, suggestions, and workspace prompts.
settings.profile.usage.tipTags: Tags are only used for preference inference. They do not replace system prompts or automatically expose data to external services.
settings.profile.usage.tipEmail: Email is maintained by the account system. This page only displays it to avoid inconsistent edits across entry points.
settings.profile.usage.inline: Details are folded into the Tips beside the title.
settings.stats.range.week.label: This Week
settings.stats.range.week.description: Focus on usage changes over the last 7 days.
settings.stats.range.month.label: This Month
settings.stats.range.month.description: Review daily usage rhythm over the last 30 days.
settings.stats.range.all.label: All Time
settings.stats.range.all.description: Review cumulative scale and long-term trends.
settings.stats.empty.noStats: No usage stats to show yet.
settings.stats.hero.title: Usage Stats
settings.stats.hero.tip: Manage token usage, active days, model distribution, and trend observations for the selected range.
settings.stats.hero.subtitle: View usage intensity, model distribution, and trends for the selected range.
settings.stats.summary.rangeTipInline: Current range details are in Tips
settings.stats.observation.title: Current Observation
settings.stats.observation.tip: Use one summary panel to quickly understand the rhythm of this period.
settings.stats.overview.title: Stage Overview
settings.stats.overview.tip: Break conversations, token usage, and estimated duration into today, this month, and all-time segments.
settings.stats.segment.today.title: Today
settings.stats.segment.today.description: Quickly judge whether current usage is high-frequency.
settings.stats.segment.month.title: This Month
settings.stats.segment.month.description: Observe normal usage rhythm over the recent month.
settings.stats.segment.total.title: All Time
settings.stats.segment.total.description: Review overall usage scale and long-term investment.
settings.stats.models.title: Model Usage Ranking
settings.stats.models.tip: See the most used models and their share in the selected range.
settings.stats.models.empty: No model usage data in the selected range yet.
settings.stats.trends.title: Daily Usage Trend
settings.stats.trends.tip: Observe daily Token fluctuations in {{range}} to identify peaks and dips.
settings.stats.trends.empty: No daily trend data in the selected range yet.
settings.stats.heatmap.title: Activity Calendar
settings.stats.heatmap.tip: View activity heat over the last 35 days. Darker cells mean more Token usage.
settings.userCenterSession.title: Account Profile
settings.userCenterSession.hero.tip: Nickname, avatar, email, and default service are maintained in Account Center; this page only shows the current session state and sync results.
settings.userCenterSession.description: Review sign-in status, default service, and account sync results.
settings.userCenterSession.empty.runtime: Cloud service is not configured for this build. The open-source build can continue using local features; branded services appear automatically after a sign-in entry is configured.
settings.userCenterSession.profile.unifi: Profile editing is unified in Account Center
settings.userCenterSession.profile.syncT: Edit profile details in Account Center. The client syncs the latest nickname, avatar, email, and default service state instead of keeping a second local profile.
settings.userCenterSession.value.email.h: From the current account information in Account Center.
settings.userCenterSession.value.identit: Used to identify the current account.
settings.userCenterSession.value.expires: You will need to sign in again after it expires.
settings.userCenterSession.value.default: The source used by default on the AI Services page.
settings.userCenterSession.profile.metho: Nickname, avatar, email, and related details are maintained in Account Center. This view focuses on account state and no longer offers a separate local profile editor.
To update profile details, edit them in Account Center, then return here and click “Sync latest state”.
settings.userCenterSession.code.sentTo: The latest verification code was sent to {{email}}.
Google is the default sign-in method. After browser authorization, the client syncs account details, default service, and enabled capabilities automatically; continue if the browser shows a confirmation page.
Sync starts after authorization in the system browser; continue if the browser shows a confirmation page.
settings.userCenterSession.login.afterBr: After Google sign-in succeeds, the desktop app syncs the current account session automatically.
If the browser shows a confirmation page, confirm the current desktop request once more.
Sync current account details, avatar, and nickname display.
Sync the default AI service, model catalog, and enabled capabilities.
Profile details stay in Account Center to avoid duplicate editing entry points.
settings.userCenterSession.login.alterna: If your organization has not enabled Google, or you need compatibility with an existing account system, use an email code or account password instead.
settings.developerLab.title: Developer & Labs
settings.developerLab.description: Keep debugging, diagnostics, and unstable capabilities together; leave them off by default, then turn them off again after use.
settings.developer.message.diagnosticCop: Diagnostics copied. You can send them directly to developers.
settings.developer.message.diagnosticHis: Old diagnostics cleared. Future copies will only include new diagnostic data.
settings.developer.message.workspaceHarn: Workspace debug collection is on. Tool inventory and environment summaries load when Harness opens.
Workspace debug collection is off. The Harness entry remains available.

settings.developer.message.clawTraceEnab: Claw trace is on. New turns record stream checkpoints; diagnostic exports include only latency summaries.
settings.developer.message.clawTraceDisa: Claw trace is off. New turns will not collect stream checkpoints.

settings.developer.message.clawTraceRegr: No regression evidence to save yet

settings.developer.message.saveClawTrace: Failed to save Claw trace snapshot
settings.developer.message.clearClawTrac: Failed to clear Claw trace history
settings.developer.message.saveClawTrace: Failed to save Claw trace regression trend
settings.developer.message.copyClawTrace: Failed to copy Claw trace regression trend
settings.developer.message.clearClawTrac: Failed to clear Claw trace regression trend

settings.developer.title: Developer
settings.developer.description: Turn these on while troubleshooting, then turn them off again.
settings.developer.debugSwitch.title: Debug Toggles
settings.developer.debugSwitch.descripti: Keep these off by default; turn them on only when inspecting layout or runtime issues.
settings.developer.debugSwitch.workspace: Collect runtime summaries, tool inventory, and environment summaries.
settings.developer.debugSwitch.clawTrace: Record Claw stream checkpoints and export only aggregated latency summaries.
0 disables collection even if the switch is on; 1 records every traced turn.

When enabled, this panel records local summary-only alert channel entries from the current attribution report and manually saved trend. It does not query App Server automatically, upload, or store raw payloads.
Toggle Claw trace regression alert channel
When enabled, newly retained summary-only alerts attempt a local desktop notification only if notification permission is already granted. Lime does not request permission automatically.
Toggle Claw trace desktop alert notification
Local history stores compact summaries only, keeps the latest {{maxRecords}} snapshots for {{maxAgeDays}} days, and excludes raw entries, prompt text, and provider payloads.
Save a Trace snapshot to compare the current summary against it.
Window: {{count}} snapshots · oldest retained baseline · latest {{latestSavedAt}}
Load at least two App Server traces in the retained window to compare summary-only checkpoints.
Window: {{count}} traces · oldest retained baseline · latest {{latestTraceId}}
Save a compact snapshot and load App Server Trace timeline to build attribution evidence.
Window: {{compactCount}} compact snapshots · {{traceCount}} App Server traces
Enable the regression alert channel to evaluate the current report and manually saved trend.
Alert channel: {{count}} saved alerts · no alert recorded
Local summary-only alert channel keeps max {{maxRecords}} records / {{maxAgeDays}} days.
Select an event to inspect summary-only details.
settings.developer.debugSwitch.component: Hold Alt and hover to see component outlines; Alt-click to inspect source.
settings.developer.diagnostic.title: Diagnostic Logs
settings.developer.diagnostic.descriptio: Copy or export the current diagnostic package; clear old records before reproducing if needed.
settings.developer.serviceSkill.title: Service Skill Catalog Debugging
settings.developer.serviceSkill.descript: View, inject, or clear the service skill catalog.
settings.developer.siteAdapter.title: Site Script Catalog Debugging
settings.developer.siteAdapter.descripti: Validate the site adapter catalog and external YAML import.
settings.developer.workspaceRepair.title: Workspace Repair History
settings.developer.workspaceRepair.descr: Review recent automatic repair and migration actions.
settings.mediaGeneration.selector.label: Default model
settings.mediaGeneration.selector.descri: Use the same model selector as chat; when unset, Lime keeps automatic matching.
settings.mediaGeneration.fallback.title: Auto fallback when Provider is unavailable
settings.mediaGeneration.image.title: Image Service Model
settings.mediaGeneration.image.descripti: Configure the default Provider, model, and fallback policy for image generation here; global parameters such as default image count stay in the AI Image settings below.
settings.mediaGeneration.image.hint.load: Only Providers that declare image-generation capability are shown.
settings.mediaGeneration.image.hint.empt: No image Provider is available. Configure a model or custom model for an image-capable service in Settings -> AI Providers first.
settings.mediaGeneration.image.hint.read: Only Providers that declare image-generation capability are shown; the future brand cloud catalog will reuse the same filter.
When disabled, Lime shows an error if the default image service is missing, disabled, or has no usable key.
settings.mediaGeneration.video.title: Video Service Model
settings.mediaGeneration.video.descripti: Configure the default Provider, model, and fallback policy for video tasks while keeping the same simple structure as image and voice.
settings.mediaGeneration.video.hint.load: Only Providers currently recognized as video-capable are shown.
settings.mediaGeneration.video.hint.empt: No video Provider is available. Configure a video-capable service in Settings -> AI Providers first.
settings.mediaGeneration.video.hint.read: Video, image, and voice settings share the same service-model skeleton, so new sources do not need a second page.
When disabled, Lime shows an error if the default video service is missing, disabled, or has no usable key.
settings.mediaServices.title: Service Models
settings.mediaServices.hero.tip: Manage the default models already wired into the main chain for naming, translation, prompt rewriting, and media generation, while reusing the same model taxonomy across local, self-managed cloud, and branded cloud providers.
settings.mediaServices.description: Only default service models that are already connected to the call chain stay here; the whole page reuses one model selector.
settings.mediaServices.common.model.empt: No model currently matches this capability. Configure a matching model under AI Providers first.
settings.mediaServices.common.inputCompl: The current input completion chain only uses the enable switch, so model selection for an unwired execution path stays hidden.
settings.mediaServices.customPrompt.desc: When filled, the system assistant keeps using this custom prompt while generating content.
settings.mediaServices.imageSettings.des: Manage the default count for image generation tasks.
settings.mediaServices.imageSettings.cou: Set the default number of images used when the image generation panel creates a new task.
settings.mediaServices.sections.responsi: Choose the low-latency model preferred for lightweight first-turn chat.
Pick a lightweight streaming chat model. When unset, the backend resolves from the current session and model metadata.
settings.mediaServices.sections.topic.de: Choose the model used to automatically name conversation topics.
settings.mediaServices.sections.topic.mo: Uses a chat or reasoning model by default; when unset, automatic selection continues to apply.
settings.mediaServices.sections.generati: Choose the model used to name image generation tasks, preferring vision-understanding models.
Vision-understanding models are shown first; when no VLM exists, Lime falls back to general chat models.
settings.mediaServices.sections.translat: Choose the model used to translate message content.
A stable chat or reasoning model is recommended.
settings.mediaServices.sections.historyC: Choose the model used to compress conversation history context.
A stable long-context chat model is recommended.
settings.mediaServices.sections.agentMet: Choose the model used to generate assistant names, descriptions, tags, and related metadata.
A general model that is good at summarizing and naming is recommended.
settings.mediaServices.sections.inputCom: Control whether input suggestions, completion panels, and quick capability hints are enabled.
The current main chain only consumes the enable switch and no longer exposes model selection for an unwired execution path.
settings.mediaServices.sections.promptRe: Choose the model used to rewrite and polish prompts.
When disabled, Lime uses the original input directly without rewriting the prompt.
settings.mediaServices.sections.resource: Choose the model used to rewrite questions with project resource context.
When disabled, project resource questions are no longer rewritten with additional context.
settings.voice.message.mediaPreferenceSa: Failed to save voice generation preference
settings.voice.processing.warning.modelU: Current polish model is not configured for {{provider}}: {{model}}
settings.voice.media.providerHint.loadin: Detecting providers available for dubbing / TTS.
settings.voice.media.providerHint.empty: No voice generation provider is available. Configure a TTS-capable service under Settings -> AI Providers first.
settings.voice.media.providerHint.ready: Only default provider, model, and fallback strategy for dubbing / voice generation are configured here.
settings.voice.processing.modelHint.empt: No chat model is available. Configure at least one LLM provider first.
settings.voice.processing.modelHint.read: Default polish and translation mode share the same model selection, reusing the chat page model selector.
settings.voice.model.message.defaultSet: {{model}} is now the default recognition service
settings.voice.model.message.defaultSetF: Failed to set {{model}} as the default model
settings.voice.model.test.pathRequired: Enter a local WAV file path first
settings.voice.model.title: Voice Models
settings.voice.model.description: Manage on-demand downloads, installation status, and default recognition service for local ASR models. Model files are written only to user data, not bundled into the app.
settings.voice.model.senseVoice.descript: Local offline ASR, downloaded on demand.
settings.voice.model.senseVoice.fallback: Local offline ASR; the model is downloaded to user data on demand.
settings.voice.model.assetBaseMissing: Download URL is not configured. Set limecore server.voiceModelAssetBaseUrl to a public CF R2 domain.
settings.voice.model.test.title: Test transcription
settings.voice.model.test.description: Choose or enter a local 16-bit PCM WAV path to verify the current SenseVoice Small installation and local inference chain. Multi-channel audio uses only the first channel.
settings.voice.model.test.needsInstall: Download and install the model before testing transcription.
settings.voice.model.emptyCatalog: No local voice model catalog is available.
settings.voice.processing.title: Voice Processing
settings.voice.processing.description: Manage default polish, translation mode, and voice instructions. Polish and translation share one LLM model selection and reuse the chat page model selector.
When enabled, regular voice input is post-processed with the default polish instruction; translation mode is not affected.
settings.voice.processing.model.label: Polish and translation model
settings.voice.processing.defaultInstruc: Regular voice input uses this instruction when AI polish is enabled.
settings.voice.processing.translateInstr: The translation mode shortcut runs the selected instruction. Prefer Translate to English or a custom translation template.
settings.voice.processing.instructionEdi: Enter the AI polish prompt template...
AI uses this prompt to polish speech recognition results.
Set a shortcut to quickly switch to this instruction mode.
Manage AI polish instructions for voice input.
settings.voice.media.title: Voice Service Model
settings.voice.media.description: Configure only the default provider, model, and fallback strategy for dubbing / voice generation tasks here. Recognition, shortcuts, and polish for voice input stay above.
settings.voice.media.selector.label: Default model
settings.voice.media.selector.descriptio: Use the same model selector as the chat page; when unset, automatic matching continues.
settings.voice.media.fallback.title: Automatically fall back when provider is unavailable
settings.voice.media.fallback.descriptio: When disabled, Lime shows an error directly if the default voice service is missing, disabled, or has no usable key.
settings.providers.cloud.message.userCen: This version has no cloud user center entry configured.
settings.providers.cloud.message.loginOp: Opened the {{brand}} login page. Complete authorization in your browser.
settings.providers.cloud.message.userCen: Opened the {{brand}} user center in your browser.
settings.providers.cloud.message.browser: Try again later, or check whether the system browser is available.
settings.providers.cloud.message.userCen: Failed to open the {{brand}} user center: {{detail}}
settings.channels.workbench.title: Logs and Checks
settings.channels.workbench.description: Only gateway, logs, and runtime checks stay here.
settings.channels.workbench.tip.scope.st: Legacy overview and configuration entries now land here to avoid exposing the same controls across multiple subpages.
Use Gateway for public entrypoints, tunnels, and callback sync. Use Logs to inspect logs, start or stop services, and query status.
Configuration forms are no longer the primary entrypoint. Troubleshooting should start here with status checks and reproducible evidence.
settings.channels.workbench.gateway.desc: Manage public tunnels, callback sync, and connectivity probes in one place.
First confirm the local gateway address (local host / port) is reachable, then configure tunnel parameters.
For Cloudflare mode, set tunnel_name and dns_name first, then run “Create tunnel”.
After the tunnel starts, run “Sync Feishu callback URL” so Feishu uses the same callback address.
After each change, query status first, then check the Logs page for connection issues.
settings.channels.workbench.logs.descrip: Inspect channel gateway and RPC logs.
settings.channels.workbench.logs.tip.ste: Start by choosing a filter mode, such as TelegramGateway, WechatGateway, or RPC, to narrow the view.
For noisy history, clear logs first, then reproduce the issue to capture a clean sample.
If no logs appear, go to Runtime and query status to confirm the service has started.
settings.channels.workbench.runtime.desc: Start, stop, query status, and handle compatibility troubleshooting.
settings.channels.gatewayTunnel.title: Gateway Public Tunnel
settings.channels.gatewayTunnel.descript: Global public webhook entrypoint shared by Feishu, Telegram, and other channels.
settings.channels.gatewayTunnel.placehol: Use cloudflared from PATH by default
⚠️ Dangerous operation detected!
Operation: system install cloudflared
Scope: calls the system package manager (brew/apt/winget, etc.) to install a global command
Risk: may request administrator permission, fail during network download, or change system package state

Confirm whether to continue?
settings.channels.gatewayRuntime.descrip: Use this for status queries, start, stop, and restart. Connectivity checks have moved into the IM configuration dialog above.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.title: WeChat Gateway Runtime Control
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.descript: Status query, start/stop, and account cleanup stay here. QR login and connectivity checks have moved into the IM configuration dialog above.
Leave Account ID empty to use the default config. For multiple accounts, specify it explicitly.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.compat.d: Expand this only when the QR flow in the IM configuration above is not working.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.field.ac: Account name to save after login (optional)
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.field.pu: Clear local cached data when deleting
First QR code generated automatically. Scan it to log in.
QR code generated. Waiting for scan confirmation in the background.
WeChat login succeeded. Gateway started automatically.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.error.no: WeChat API did not return a usable QR code
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.error.mi: Generate a QR code first, or enter an existing session_key
, and local cache and sync state will be deleted
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.qr.ready: Scan with WeChat and confirm on your phone. This page will automatically wait for login and write the config back.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.qr.local: This page renders the QR payload returned by WeChat locally and does not rely on remote image loading.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.qr.empty: Click Generate QR Code first. If the API does not return QR content, Latest Result below will show the error.
settings.channels.wechatRuntime.accounts: Accounts visible after QR login or manual configuration are listed here.
No WeChat account yet. Use WeChat configuration above to connect by QR first; use this compatibility troubleshooting area only when the main flow fails.
settings.channels.logTail.title: Channel Log Tail
settings.channels.logTail.description: Sources: runtime in-memory logs plus lime.log and rotated logs from the app log directory, refreshed every second.
settings.channels.logTail.error.invalidR: Invalid regex. Falling back to no filter.
settings.channels.logTail.message.noCopy: No logs to copy in the current view
settings.channels.logTail.message.copyPe: Copy failed. Check system clipboard permission.
settings.channels.logTail.confirm.clear:
Clear logs?
This will clear current in-memory logs, the current log file, and historical channel diagnostic logs. This cannot be undone.
settings.channels.logTail.empty.paused: No matching logs. Refresh is paused.
settings.channels.logTail.empty.waiting: No matching logs yet. Waiting for new logs.
settings.system.workspaceRepair.title: Workspace Auto-Repair History
settings.system.workspaceRepair.descript: Records recent auto-repair and migration actions without interrupting your work.
No repair record to copy yet
Copy failed. Check the window focus or system clipboard permission.
settings.agent.skills.advancedEntry.tip: The left Claw navigation already provides the end-user Skills entry. This settings page only keeps advanced capabilities such as local import, repository management, and standard checks.
settings.home.title: Settings Home
settings.home.description: Quickly open common settings and review each group.
settings.home.hero.tip: Quickly open common settings and review each group without digging through nested menus.
settings.home.quickAccess.title: Quick Access
settings.home.quickAccess.tip: Keep the most-used pages on the first screen, then drill into detailed settings.
settings.home.quickAccess.description: Keep frequent pages on the first screen to reduce jumps inside Settings.
settings.home.quickAccess.appearance.des: Theme, interface language, and reply language
Manage assistant, media, and speech models in one place
settings.home.current.title: Current Entrypoints
settings.home.current.tip: The foreground keeps only current Skills; these are the active primary and auxiliary paths.
settings.home.current.description: Foreground entrypoints now converge here and open the latest paths directly.
settings.home.current.automation.title: Automation
settings.home.current.automation.descrip: Open it from System to focus on job creation, runs, and continuation.
settings.home.current.channels.title: Message Channels
settings.home.current.channels.descripti: Open it from System to manage external message connections and channel setup.
settings.home.current.resources.title: Project Knowledge
settings.home.current.resources.descript: Kept as an auxiliary page for browsing and organizing project knowledge; default knowledge navigation returns to Project Resources and the generation path.
settings.home.group.account.description: Profile, usage stats, and account information.
settings.home.group.general.description: Appearance, memory, and global experience settings.
settings.home.group.agent.description: Unified provider, skill, and media capability settings.
settings.home.group.system.description: Connectors, MCP, environment variables, and runtime governance.
settings.environment.hero.description: Manage Shell imports, explicit overrides, and the final environment preview.
settings.environment.hero.tip: Manage Shell imports, explicit overrides, and the final environment preview. Sensitive values stay masked by default to reduce accidental exposure in Settings.
settings.environment.hero.title: Environment Variables
settings.environment.merge.compat.tip: Legacy entries such as Web Search can still be edited, but they all converge into the same environment config to avoid conflicting sources.
settings.environment.merge.compat.title: Compatibility entries
settings.environment.merge.description: Review the priority order before deciding whether a variable belongs in the Shell or in explicit overrides.
settings.environment.merge.priority1.tip: Explicit environment overrides replace same-name values directly, which is useful for proxies, API hosts, or debug switches.
settings.environment.merge.priority2.tip: Shell import inherits PATH, proxy, and toolchain variables from the login environment, reducing duplicate entry.
settings.environment.merge.title: Merge Rules
settings.environment.message.saved: Environment settings were saved and applied to the current runtime
settings.environment.overrides.descripti: Set or override runtime environment variables explicitly. These take priority over Shell import and legacy compatibility logic.
settings.environment.overrides.empty.des: Only Shell import and runtime-derived variables are in use right now, which is a good starting point for observing the effective result.
settings.environment.overrides.title: Environment Overrides
settings.environment.preview.description: Shows the key variables the unified environment layer will provide to the runtime and where each value currently comes from.
settings.environment.preview.empty.descr: After you save the config, Lime recalculates the unified environment layer so you can confirm the final value and source here.
settings.environment.preview.empty.title: No environment variables to preview
settings.environment.preview.title: Effective Preview
settings.environment.shellImport.descrip: Optionally read PATH, proxy, and version-manager environment from the login Shell, then merge it with explicit overrides.
settings.environment.shellImport.duratio: Use this to judge whether the current Shell import is too slow.
Useful when Lime needs PATH, proxy, Node version manager, or similar login Shell environment values.
settings.environment.shellImport.importe: Number of environment variables collected from the Shell right now.
settings.environment.shellImport.preview: Shell environment import is disabled. Lime only uses the current process environment and explicit overrides.
Saving will recalculate the current unified environment layer.
Imported {{count}} variables from the Shell environment.
Shell environment import timed out after {{duration}} and fell back to explicit overrides only.
Import status is not available yet. Save to view the final result.
settings.environment.shellImport.timeout: If the import times out, Lime falls back to explicit overrides only and does not block the runtime.
settings.environment.shellImport.title: Shell Environment Import
Shell import depends on differences between the current system Shell and login environment. Use explicit overrides first when you need stable results.
settings.environment.tips.description: This page is mainly for desktop scenarios that need stable runtime environment management.
settings.environment.tips.mask.tip: The preview masks values by default. Sensitive content is shown directly only after you explicitly click Show values.
settings.environment.tips.mask.title: Sensitive values are masked by default
settings.environment.tips.preview.tip: If you only need to override a few variables, add explicit overrides first, then confirm the source in the preview below.
settings.environment.tips.preview.title: Override first, then preview

settings.webSearch.description: Manage search engines, provider fallback, and image search keys.

settings.system.clipboardPermission.maco: Click anywhere in the Lime window, then try copying again.
Open System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Accessibility and confirm Lime is enabled.
If it still fails, use Export Diagnostic JSON and send it to the developers.
settings.system.clipboardPermission.wind: Click anywhere in the Lime window, then try copying again.
Open Settings -> Privacy & security -> Clipboard and confirm system clipboard features are available.
If enterprise policy restricts clipboard access, use Export Diagnostic JSON instead.
settings.system.clipboardPermission.linu: Click anywhere in the Lime window, then try copying again.
Check your desktop privacy settings and confirm the app is not blocked from clipboard access.
If it still fails on Wayland, use Export Diagnostic JSON.
settings.system.clipboardPermission.gene: Click anywhere in the Lime window, then try copying again.
Check system privacy settings and confirm the app is not blocked from clipboard access.
If it still fails, use Export Diagnostic JSON.
settings.memory.title: Memory
settings.memory.hero.description: Control whether Lime remembers project knowledge, preferences, and communication style. Everyday settings and advanced tools are separated.
settings.memory.everyday.title: Everyday memory status
settings.memory.everyday.description: Check whether Lime can retrieve confirmed project knowledge and long-term preferences. Model source details live in Advanced.
settings.memory.store.resetConfirm: Reset memory files? This removes MEMORY.md, memory_summary.md, notes, and index files from the file store. AI personality stays unchanged.
settings.memory.store.message.healthFail: Failed to load memory file status
settings.memory.store.message.consolidat: Memory notes consolidated. Processed {{processed}}, sent {{skipped}} to review.
settings.memory.store.message.resetDone: Memory files reset. Removed {{files}} files and {{directories}} folders.
settings.memory.store.noteDescription: Save a confirmed preference, correction, or long-term constraint as a short note. It goes into file-backed correction notes and does not rewrite the summary immediately.
settings.memory.store.notePlaceholder: Example: When answering product plans, explain tradeoffs before giving the recommendation.
settings.memory.store.message.noteEmpty: Enter a memory correction to save
settings.memory.store.rolloutEmpty: No run summary candidates in the default workspace.
settings.memory.store.rolloutTruncated: Preview truncated. Open the memory file for the full content.
settings.memory.store.message.rolloutLoa: Failed to load run summary candidates
settings.memory.store.message.rolloutWor: Default workspace is unavailable, so run summary candidates cannot be consolidated
settings.memory.store.message.rolloutCon: Run summary candidates consolidated. Processed {{processed}}, sent {{skipped}} to review.
Failed to consolidate run summary candidates
Prefer local all-MiniLM-L6-v2, try Ollama when unavailable, then fall back to full-text retrieval.
settings.memory.embedding.provider.local: Use the local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding model without cloud API dependency.
settings.memory.embedding.provider.ollam: Use a local Ollama embedding model for memory vectors. nomic-embed-text is recommended.
settings.memory.embedding.provider.opena: Use the OpenAI Provider Embeddings API.
settings.memory.embedding.provider.disab: Disable vector retrieval and keep title and summary full-text retrieval only.
settings.memory.embedding.status.configu: Search vectors will be generated from the current memory store.
settings.memory.embedding.status.fullTex: Only full-text retrieval is enabled; no vector index is generated.
settings.memory.embedding.providerSelect: Most users only need to choose the model source. Lime handles connection details and storage automatically.
settings.memory.toggle.syncedHint: The current config matches the saved version.
settings.memory.message.restored: Restored to the last saved version
settings.memory.soul.title: AI personality
settings.memory.soul.description: Start from an initial template. Later preferences keep improving through guided onboarding and conversations.
settings.memory.soul.current.title: Current AI personality
settings.memory.soul.current.description: The current draft will guide chat interaction after saving.
settings.memory.soul.current.emptyDescri: Choose a template as the starting point, or import a SOUL.md draft in Advanced.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.title: Interaction style
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.descri: Choose how Lime speaks during chat, tool progress, follow-up questions, and failure recovery. Formal artifacts still use Generation Brief.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.seriou: High-risk, permission, deletion, production API, medical, legal, and financial situations automatically use the calm professional style.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.missin: Saved profile "{{profileId}}" is not installed or enabled. Lime will temporarily use "{{fallbackProfileId}}" and restore it after the style pack is available again.
The current interaction style is unavailable, so Lime switched back to the default style. Save to apply.
settings.memory.soul.stylePacks.title: Local style packs
settings.memory.soul.stylePacks.descript: Import, enable, disable, or uninstall personal style packs. Available profiles appear in the selector above.
settings.memory.soul.stylePacks.empty: No personal style packs imported yet. The four built-in styles remain available.
settings.memory.soul.stylePacks.import.m: Select a manifest JSON and five locale JSON files.
settings.memory.soul.stylePacks.import.r: Failed to read the style pack files. Select them again.
Failed to read local style packs.
Failed to update style pack status.
settings.memory.soul.stylePack.builtIn.d: The default Soul interaction style set shipped with the app, including cheeky, warm, cool, and calm professional styles.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.cheeky: A little cheeky, lightly teasing, and execution-oriented. It should come through in rhythm and specifics, not fixed catchphrases or crude jokes.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.warmSu: Patient, low-pressure, and steady. Good for planning, reflection, and writing blocks without forcing comfort phrases into every reply.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.coolCo: Crisp, composed, and decisive. Good for fast execution, result handoff, and review summaries without becoming arrogant or bossy.
settings.memory.soul.styleProfile.calmPr: Concise, auditable, and risk-aware. Good for coding, research, and high-stakes topics.
settings.memory.soul.template.title: Initial templates
settings.memory.soul.template.descriptio: Templates provide a starting point without asking users to write prompt fields.
settings.memory.soul.template.balanced.d: For everyday collaboration: answer first, then add the key evidence.
settings.memory.soul.template.balanced.s: Lead with the answer, then add key evidence. Keep the tone professional, restrained, and easy to work with over time.
settings.memory.soul.template.balanced.a: Do not present uncertainty as fact
settings.memory.soul.template.direct.des: For engineering, product, and plan reviews: surface risks and weak assumptions first.
settings.memory.soul.template.direct.sum: Point out problems, risks, and weak assumptions directly, then offer executable alternatives.
settings.memory.soul.template.direct.com: Call out blockers and risks first
settings.memory.soul.template.direct.avo: Do not soften key issues just to sound polite
Do not add explanations with no action value
settings.memory.soul.template.creator.de: For content ideas and editing: preserve the point of view while improving readability.
settings.memory.soul.template.creator.su: Help structure expression, preserve real judgment, and polish drafts into natural, opinionated writing.
settings.memory.soul.template.creator.co: Extract the core claim and reader effect first
Preserve the user's own judgment and voice
settings.memory.soul.template.creator.av: Do not turn writing into marketing copy
Do not make ordinary language sound translated
settings.memory.soul.import.title: Import SOUL.md
settings.memory.soul.import.description: Paste an OpenClaw or Hermes style SOUL.md. Import only creates a config draft; the file path will not become a runtime source.
settings.memory.soul.import.warning.empt: The content is empty and will not overwrite the current config.
settings.memory.soul.import.warning.proj: Project rules or commands were detected. Move them to project rules or knowledge instead.
settings.memory.soul.import.warning.loca: Local paths were detected. Imported config will not keep path dependencies.
settings.memory.soul.import.warning.secr: Possible secrets or tokens were detected. Remove them before importing.
settings.memory.soul.import.warning.tooL: The content is long, so the preview was truncated at the import limit.
settings.memory.soul.export.title: SOUL.md file content
settings.memory.soul.export.description: View the SOUL.md migration text generated from the current draft. This content is only for viewing and migration.
settings.memory.soul.export.empty: There is no SOUL.md content to view yet.
settings.memory.soul.boundary.title: Boundary
settings.memory.soul.boundary.descriptio: Formal content writing style is selected in the creation flow. Voice IDs are not filled here.
settings.memory.soul.message.templateApp: Personality template applied. Save to take effect.
settings.memory.soul.message.styleProfil: Interaction style updated. Save to take effect.
settings.memory.soul.message.importAppli: Applied to draft. Save to take effect.
settings.memory.soul.message.exportEmpty: No SOUL.md is available to export
settings.automation.focus.title: Continue this one first
settings.automation.focus.label: Continue this one first
settings.automation.focus.description: Only the most worth-continuing practice and next step are lifted out of ongoing flows.
settings.automation.focus.empty: No ongoing practice is ready to continue yet. Once a flow actually runs with results, it will be linked here automatically.
settings.automation.health.title: Risk Alerts
settings.automation.health.description: These stats are only assistive. Prioritize ongoing flows waiting for human action, failed, or in cooldown.
settings.automation.health.empty: No high-risk ongoing flows right now.
settings.automation.main.load.error.sche: Failed to load ongoing flow scheduler config
settings.automation.main.load.error.heal: Failed to load ongoing flow health
settings.automation.main.load.errorDescr: The ongoing flow scheduler config is temporarily unavailable. Try again later.
settings.automation.main.toast.partialLo: Some ongoing flow data failed to load: {{details}}
settings.automation.main.hero.descriptio: Manage the global scheduler toggle, polling interval, and run history retention here. Starting flows and recent runs continue on the ongoing flows page.
Turn practices worth following into long-running follow-up, currently focused on ongoing flows brought back from Agent conversations.
Turn practices worth following into long-running follow-up, with one place to view flows, runs, and scheduler settings.
settings.automation.main.hero.summary.se: Manage the scheduler toggle, polling interval, and history retention.
settings.automation.main.hero.summary.wo: Focus on starting flows, recent runs, and overview switching.
settings.automation.main.hero.summary.fu: View ongoing flows, run status, and scheduler settings together.
settings.automation.overview.title: Run Overview
settings.automation.overview.tipContent: Stats, health, and risk alerts are collected here instead of staying on the ongoing flow start screen.
settings.automation.scheduler.title: Scheduler Settings
settings.automation.scheduler.descriptio: Keep only the global toggle, polling interval, and history retention here. Starting flows and run handling no longer mix into settings.
settings.automation.scheduler.enable.des: Poll the automation_jobs table in the background
settings.automation.scheduler.history.de: Write to the unified agent_runs table
settings.automation.tasks.title: Start this ongoing flow first
settings.automation.tasks.description: The default page keeps only ongoing flow actions brought back from Agent conversations. Templates prefill cadence, starting prompt, and delivery destination; browser automation no longer has a separate starter entry.
settings.automation.tasks.legacyBrowserN: {{count}} legacy browser flows were detected. They are disabled and will no longer launch Chrome in the background. Delete them and rebuild with Agent conversation ongoing flows.
settings.automation.tasks.template.daily: Summarize key progress in this content chain at a fixed time every day.
Useful for daily reports, morning briefs, and inspection summaries.
Generate a concise summary on a fixed schedule
Summarize key progress, exceptions, and follow-ups from the recent cycle. Produce a concise summary.
settings.automation.tasks.template.struc: Organize results into structured output, then deliver them to files or external channels.
Useful for webhooks, local files, and spreadsheet sync.
Generate and deliver structured results on a fixed cadence
Organize this run into a structured summary and keep the key fields.
settings.automation.tasks.template.blank: Define cadence, starting prompt, and delivery destination from scratch without template constraints.
Best for people already familiar with ongoing flow configuration.
settings.automation.tasks.list.title: Running Ongoing Flows
settings.automation.tasks.list.descripti: Each ongoing flow has an owner, cadence, and starting prompt. Open details when you need recent runs, delivery destination, or more context.
settings.automation.tasks.empty.title: No ongoing flows yet
settings.automation.tasks.empty.descript: Start your first ongoing flow here. Future scheduled continuations will stay on this main path.
settings.automation.tasks.toast.runCompl: Run complete: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed, {{timeout}} timed out
settings.automation.history.title: Recent Runs
settings.automation.jobDialog.accessMode: The final policy is on-request + workspace-write.
The final policy is never + danger-full-access.
The final policy is on-request + read-only.
settings.automation.jobDialog.delivery.b: When off, a delivery failure also marks this run as failed.
Delivery failure does not block this run
settings.automation.jobDialog.delivery.d: Google Sheets connects with a service account. Target format: spreadsheet_id=...;sheet=...;credentials_file=absolute path. Optional include_header=true and value_input_option=USER_ENTERED; appended rows include metadata columns such as delivery_attempt_id.
Local files are useful for a first minimal output loop; text is rendered by contract and json writes structured results.
Telegram remains a compatible notification channel. It sends text notifications only and does not promise a structured output contract.
Webhook suits system integration. It sends output_schema, output_format, structured output_data, and a stable delivery_attempt_id idempotency key.
settings.automation.jobDialog.delivery.t: Absolute path for the output file
settings.automation.jobDialog.descriptio: Configure the flow name, cadence, starting prompt, and output destination.
View the historical configuration snapshot and migrate it to an Agent conversation ongoing flow.
settings.automation.jobDialog.field.desc: Describe the outcome expected after this ongoing flow runs
settings.automation.jobDialog.field.name: Flow Name
Example: Daily brand lead inspection
settings.automation.jobDialog.field.prom: Describe what this ongoing flow should do each time it starts
settings.automation.jobDialog.field.syst: Optional. Control the execution style for this ongoing flow
settings.automation.jobDialog.legacy.mes: Browser automation has been retired, and the system will no longer start Chrome automatically. Delete this legacy flow and rebuild it with an Agent conversation ongoing flow.
settings.automation.jobDialog.legacy.sna: This configuration is kept for display only and cannot be saved or executed.
settings.automation.jobDialog.legacy.sum: Use the profile default start URL
settings.automation.jobDialog.schedule.h: One-time trigger. It is disabled automatically after the time arrives.
Use a Cron expression to drive execution.
Poll on a fixed second-level interval.
settings.automation.jobDialog.tip.create: Use this ongoing flow to continue a proven Agent conversation workflow, with one place to manage cadence, workspace, output destination, and run history.
settings.automation.jobDialog.tip.legacy: Browser automation has been retired. This dialog only keeps historical configuration for display and migration reference, and it cannot be saved.
settings.automation.jobDialog.toggle.ena: When off, this ongoing flow no longer participates in polling.
settings.automation.jobDialog.toggle.web: Enable search capability just for this ongoing flow.
settings.automation.jobDialog.validation: The one-time trigger time is invalid.
Polling interval cannot be less than 60 seconds.
Max retries cannot be less than 1.
Create ongoing flows from a current project thread first.
Timeout must be a positive integer.

settings.automation.details.legacy.title: Browser automation has been retired
settings.automation.details.sceneApp.ret: This ongoing flow came from the legacy standalone SceneApp runtime. Lime now keeps only the historical task details and no longer backfills practice summaries or launches the old runtime chain.
settings.automation.details.serviceSkill: This keeps the legacy catalog compatibility marker, but execution still runs on the client.
settings.automation.details.tip: Review this ongoing flow's status, output destination, and recent runs. When migrating legacy browser flows, confirm the legacy configuration and risk notice here too.

settings.providers.modelAdd.catalog.apiH: Fill in the API Base URL from local settings
settings.providers.modelAdd.catalog.regi: Model catalog provider. Add the API Base URL from the service docs.

settings.providers.modelAdd.catalog.temp: Kimi Code Membership (Subscription)
Official Kimi Code subscription endpoint with the Anthropic protocol, suited for Claude Code and similar coding clients.
Kimi API (China PAYG)
Moonshot China Anthropic-compatible API for pay-as-you-go access to Kimi models.
Kimi API (Global PAYG)
Moonshot international Anthropic-compatible API for overseas pay-as-you-go accounts.
MiniMax Coding Plan (China)
MiniMax China Anthropic-compatible coding plan, defaulting to MiniMax-M2.7.
MiniMax Coding Plan (Global)
MiniMax international Anthropic-compatible coding plan using the overseas subscription endpoint.
GLM Coding Plan (China)
Zhipu BigModel China Anthropic / Claude API-compatible coding endpoint.
Z.AI Coding Plan (Global)
Z.AI international Anthropic / Claude API-compatible coding endpoint.
MiMo Coding Plan
Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan with a Claude Code-compatible Anthropic protocol.
Alibaba Coding Plan (China)
Alibaba Cloud Bailian China Anthropic endpoint dedicated to Claude Code Coding Plan.
Alibaba Coding Plan (Global)
Alibaba Cloud Model Studio international Anthropic endpoint dedicated to Claude Code Coding Plan.
Agnes
Official Agnes image generation endpoint using the OpenAI-compatible API, defaulting to agnes-image-2.1-flash.
AiHubMix
A common relay service that aggregates Claude, OpenAI, and Gemini models.
OpenRouter
An overseas model aggregation platform with flexible model ID access.

settings.appearance.hero.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.hero.tip: Manage theme, interface language, reply language, and recommended-question context.
settings.appearance.hero.description: Manage theme, interface language, reply language, and recommendation behavior.
settings.appearance.base.title: Base Appearance
settings.appearance.base.description: Set the global theme, interface language, and reply language before tuning the workspace rhythm.
settings.appearance.theme.title: Theme Mode
settings.appearance.theme.tip: Controls the light/dark feel across the app and adapts to different environments.
settings.appearance.colorScheme.title: Global Skin
settings.appearance.colorScheme.tip: Changes the app shell, sidebar, stage, cards, and composer together; Theme Mode still controls light and dark appearance.
settings.appearance.colorScheme.random.d: Switch to a random global skin each time.
settings.appearance.responseLanguage.tip: Controls the default language for chat replies. It does not change the interface language, browser site language, or content target language.
settings.appearance.responseLanguage.opt: Choose the reply language from recent input and context.
Reply in Simplified Chinese by default.
Reply in Traditional Chinese by default.
settings.appearance.recommendation.title: Recommendation Behavior
settings.appearance.recommendation.descr: Control whether recommended questions include the current selection to reduce repeated copy-paste.
settings.appearance.recommendation.inner: Control whether homepage recommendations include the current selection to reduce repeated copy-paste.
settings.appearance.recommendation.appen: When a document or canvas has a selection, recommendations include it as context automatically.
Toggle appending selected text to recommendations
Best for daytime and bright environments.
settings.appearance.theme.options.dark.d: Reduces glare when working at night.
settings.appearance.theme.options.system: Follows the operating system appearance automatically.
Dream Blossom
Dreamy blossom character, pink petals, and wine-red accents.
Forest Sanctuary

Red White Future City

settings.executionPolicy.title: Execution Policy
settings.executionPolicy.description: Manage workspace sandboxing, command risk approval, and network rules for code tasks so blocked runs can be recovered from one place.
settings.executionPolicy.hero.tip: These settings write to the current config and flow into the App Server current main path for later code runs.
settings.executionPolicy.workspace.title: Workspace Sandbox
settings.executionPolicy.workspace.descr: Keep commands inside the current workspace boundary by default and show recovery hints when fallback is needed.
settings.executionPolicy.workspace.enabl: Code tasks run inside the current workspace boundary first to reduce accidental external path edits.
settings.executionPolicy.workspace.stric: Require commands and file operations to stay inside the workspace boundary. Useful for high-risk repos and real client work.
settings.executionPolicy.workspace.notif: Keep a visible reminder in diagnostics when the runtime has to fall back without sandboxing.
settings.executionPolicy.shell.title: Shell Command Approval
settings.executionPolicy.shell.descripti: Control Bash risk warnings and add rules for common dangerous commands.
settings.executionPolicy.network.title: Network Rules
settings.executionPolicy.network.descrip: Add risk rules for downloads, external requests, or specific hosts so diagnostics can explain blocks directly.
settings.executionPolicy.rules.shell.emp: No Shell command rules yet. Keep the default risk warning and add rules only for explicit high-risk commands.
settings.executionPolicy.rules.network.e: No network rules yet. Add rules for external downloads, intranet hosts, or high-risk domains.
settings.executionPolicy.rules.field.rea: Explain why a warning or block is needed
settings.executionPolicy.summary.title: Current Policy Summary
settings.executionPolicy.sources.title: Policy Source Layers
settings.executionPolicy.sources.descrip: Runs merge default policy, current config, organization, user, runtime, and request policy in order; later sources win.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.default: The built-in tool catalog provides baseline restrictions and default risk levels.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.persist: This page saves {{shellRules}} command rules, {{networkRules}} network rules, and {{toolOverrides}} tool overrides.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.organiz: Injected from organizationExecutionPolicy in request metadata; this page does not write it directly.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.user.de: Injected from userExecutionPolicy and can override part of the organization layer.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.runtime: Injected from executionPolicy / harness.executionPolicy, usually by the active task or runner.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.request: Injected from requestExecutionPolicy, has the highest priority, and only affects the current request.
settings.executionPolicy.sources.note: This page edits only the “current config” layer. Organization, user, runtime, and request layers are shown as sources in runtime diagnostics and are not faked as local saves here.
settings.executionPolicy.focus.networkRu: No network rule matches {{value}}. Add one below and save it.
settings.executionPolicy.recoveryNote: When a code task is blocked by sandboxing, Shell risk, or network policy, the reliability panel points here for recovery settings.

settings.mcpPage.help.title: What is MCP?
settings.mcpPage.help.protocol: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an AI tool extension protocol that lets AI access external resources such as file systems and databases.
settings.mcpPage.help.sync: After adding MCP servers here, you can sync them to Claude, Local CLI, and Gemini CLI.
settings.mcpPage.help.import: You can also import existing MCP configuration from those tools and manage it in one place.
